title_en: Zynq FPGA Low Latency H.264 Design Solution (Encoding + Decoding < 1ms)
text:
Achieving sub-millisecond video encoding latency typically means sacrificing system flexibility or footprint. But what if you could have both?

This post details how FPGA-based H.264 codecs deliver high-performance, low-latency video solutions for demanding applications.

⚡ Sub-millisecond Latency: Achieves H.264 encoding latency as low as 0.5ms.
⚙️ Pure Hardware Implementation: FPGA-based IP core ensures minimal overhead and negligible startup time.
🔄 Integrated System Design: Combines processing and FPGA logic on a single device, optimizing footprint and performance for video streaming.

For applications like real-time industrial monitoring, autonomous systems, or secure communications, this blend of speed and compact integration is critical for reliable operation.

Our team at JYTech has been building robust, low-latency video processing solutions on this architecture through our Sienovo product line.

Full write-up: https://sienovo.jytech.us/blog/122848777

What are your biggest video latency challenges in current deployments?
